title: Add Unit Economics pipeline Grafana dashboards + freshness/completeness alerts
preview:
## Summary
Adds internal Grafana dashboards and alert rules for the Unit Economics pipeline (hourly aggregation + warehouse views) so we can detect missing hours, freshness lag, and dimension coverage regressions before Finance/Product consume the Console dashboard.

This PR lives in `observability-pack` and only sh...
text:
## Summary
Adds internal Grafana dashboards and alert rules for the Unit Economics pipeline (hourly aggregation + warehouse views) so we can detect missing hours, freshness lag, and dimension coverage regressions before Finance/Product consume the Console dashboard.

This PR lives in `observability-pack` and only ships dashboards/alerts (no runtime or pipeline code changes). It assumes the aggregator and DQ checks emit the metrics introduced in the Redwood monorepo work (ENG-8426/ENG-8429).

## Motivation
During dogfood we repeatedly saw "looks like a cost spike" that was actually missing output tokens or delayed aggregation. The Console page is not the right place to diagnose pipeline health, and SRE asked for a standard set of panels + alerts aligned to the proposed SLO.

## What’s included
- Grafana dashboard: **Unit Economics — Pipeline Health**
 - Freshness lag (now - last succes...

title: Add canary check for output-token metering parity (streaming + non-streaming)
preview: Context: we’ve had at least one production regression where streaming responses under-counted output tokens in metering, which then cascaded into Unit Economics dashboards (blended $/1M output tokens) and reconciliation views. This PR adds an automated canary that detects output-token metering regressions before a g...
text:
Context: we’ve had at least one production regression where streaming responses under-counted output tokens in metering, which then cascaded into Unit Economics dashboards (blended $/1M output tokens) and reconciliation views. This PR adds an automated canary that detects output-token metering regressions before a gateway/runtime change is widely rolled out.

What this adds:
- New canary suite `metering_output_token_parity` that runs against a small allowlisted canary tenant in prod.
- Exercises BOTH streaming and non-streaming generation paths (including tool-call shaped outputs) and validates:
 1) output_tokens reported by the API response metadata
 2) output_tokens recorded by metering events (usage pipeline)
 3) that the delta between (1) and (2) is within a configured tolerance after accounting for ingestion latency
- Adds guardrails for ingestion lag: the canary polls the meteri...

title: Add data quality checks + anomaly alerts for unit economics pipeline
preview: Context: We’ve seen multiple cases where the Unit Economics dashboard showed sudden improvements or drops that were actually pipeline issues (most commonly ingestion lag or missing dimensions), e.g. the “hours missing” incident thread and earlier streaming token undercount investigations. This PR adds first-pass dat...
text:
Context: We’ve seen multiple cases where the Unit Economics dashboard showed sudden improvements or drops that were actually pipeline issues (most commonly ingestion lag or missing dimensions), e.g. the “hours missing” incident thread and earlier streaming token undercount investigations. This PR adds first-pass data quality checks and anomaly alerts for the hourly unit economics aggregation outputs so we can detect regressions quickly and avoid Finance/Product interpreting bad data.

What this PR does: (1) Completeness checks per hour: verifies presence of expected rows for core dimensions (region, customer_tier, model) and flags coverage below threshold; (2) Missing dimension checks: alerts when unknown/NULL region or customer_tier exceeds a configured percentage; (3) Token-volume anomaly checks: compares output_tokens to a trailing baseline (7d median by {region,tier} and global) a...

title: Add internal feature flag + RBAC gating for Unit Economics dashboard
preview: Context: The new internal Unit Economics dashboard surfaces sensitive cost data (blended $/1M output tokens, drilldowns by model/region/tier). We need to ensure access is restricted to a small internal allowlist initially and that all access is auditable for Finance and Security sign-off. This PR adds (1) a server-s...
text: Context: The new internal Unit Economics dashboard surfaces sensitive cost data (blended $/1M output tokens, drilldowns by model/region/tier). We need to ensure access is restricted to a small internal allowlist initially and that all access is auditable for Finance and Security sign-off. This PR adds (1) a server-side feature flag gate and (2) RBAC policy updates + enforcement in the Console backend so that the Unit Economics endpoints and UI route are not reachable unless explicitly granted. Changes: - Feature flag: add `console.internal_unit_economics_dashboard` (server-side, env-scoped). Default OFF in all environments; can be enabled in prod only for the internal allowlist group. - RBAC: introduce permission `console:unit_economics:read` and require it for all unit economics API endpoints + the console route loader. - Enforcement points: apply gating at the HTTP router layer and ...

title: Backfill tooling for 2025Q1 unit economics aggregates (idempotent + throttled)
preview: Context: Finance needs QTD and quarter-close reporting in the internal Unit Economics dashboard, but the hourly aggregation job only covered data from the initial cutover onward. This PR adds an idempotent backfill tool to populate historical aggregates for 2025Q1 (and generally any arbitrary time window) while prot...
text: Context: Finance needs QTD and quarter-close reporting in the internal Unit Economics dashboard, but the hourly aggregation job only covered data from the initial cutover onward. This PR adds an idempotent backfill tool to populate historical aggregates for 2025Q1 (and generally any arbitrary time window) while protecting the warehouse and avoiding double-counting. Scope: (1) Adds a backfill CLI (unit-econ-backfill) that replays the same aggregation logic as the hourly job over a time range; (2) Implements idempotency via per-hour partition overwrite semantics and a backfill_runs audit table; (3) Adds throttling (max concurrent partitions, adaptive sleep on warehouse pressure) and a maintenance-window mode; (4) Adds verification queries and a reconciliation helper view to compare pre/post backfill totals; (5) Updates runbook docs referenced by alerts. Idempotency model: the backfill o...

title: Unit economics: add rate limits + response caching for dashboard query APIs
preview: Summary: Adds server-side caching and request rate limiting to the internal Unit Economics dashboard query APIs introduced in PR #19374. This is to (1) protect the warehouse/materialized views from repeated identical queries (especially top-N + timeseries defaults), (2) reduce load during Finance/exec review windows...
text: Summary: Adds server-side caching and request rate limiting to the internal Unit Economics dashboard query APIs introduced in PR #19374. This is to (1) protect the warehouse/materialized views from repeated identical queries (especially top-N + timeseries defaults), (2) reduce load during Finance/exec review windows, and (3) improve dashboard responsiveness for common filters. Context: During dogfood we saw heavy burst traffic from the Console UI due to filter changes + auto-refresh + multiple panels firing in parallel. In the worst case, a single user interaction triggered 6-10 warehouse queries with identical group_by/filter payloads (only differing by panel). This PR implements deterministic cache keys and a small set of safe defaults that do not change correctness semantics (cached responses are bounded by short TTLs and include freshness metadata). Design notes: 1) Two cache prof...

title: Add runbook links + remediation hints to unit economics alert payloads
preview:
Context

We’ve had a couple of "unknown page / where do I start" escalations when the Unit Economics pipeline alerts fire (freshness/completeness/anomaly checks). The alerts today include the alert name, labels, and a few raw counters, but they don’t provide:
- a direct runbook link
- the most common remediation ste...
text:
Context

We’ve had a couple of "unknown page / where do I start" escalations when the Unit Economics pipeline alerts fire (freshness/completeness/anomaly checks). The alerts today include the alert name, labels, and a few raw counters, but they don’t provide:
- a direct runbook link
- the most common remediation steps
- a pointer to validation queries / backfill command entrypoints

This PR updates the alert payloads emitted by the economics pipeline (and the alert templates used by the internal notifier) to include runbook links + short remediation hints so first responders can take action without context switching.

What’s in this PR

1) Alert payload schema enhancements
- Adds optional fields to the canonical alert payload:
 - runbook_url (string)
 - remediation (short bullet-like string; kept intentionally brief)
 - dashboard_url (string; used when we have a Grafana panel / Consol...

title: Bugfix: preserve rows when region mapping is null/unknown in unit economics aggregation
preview: Context: After merging the region normalization work, we noticed intermittent drops in hourly unit economics aggregates where a small (but non-trivial) slice of metering rows had region unset or had a value that didn’t map (e.g. legacy colo labels, nulls from older gateway paths, or "unknown" emitted during partial ...
text:
Context: After merging the region normalization work, we noticed intermittent drops in hourly unit economics aggregates where a small (but non-trivial) slice of metering rows had region unset or had a value that didn’t map (e.g. legacy colo labels, nulls from older gateway paths, or "unknown" emitted during partial outages). The aggregation job used an INNER JOIN to the canonical region mapping table, which caused those rows to be dropped entirely. This under-counted output_tokens and made $/1M output tokens look artificially worse/better depending on which spend inputs were present for that hour.

What this PR does: Update the unit economics aggregation query and the region mapping helper to be null-safe and non-dropping: (1) switch the join to LEFT JOIN, (2) COALESCE to a canonical region value of "unknown" when mapping fails, and (3) emit an explicit counter for unmapped rows so we...

title: Backpressure controls + max lag safety (auto-pause) for downstream outages
preview: Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit bac...
text:
Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit backpressure controls and a max-lag safety mechanism to keep the system stable during downstream outages while preserving at-least-once semantics.

What’s in this PR: (1) Max lag threshold per destination with auto-pause: if export_lag_seconds for a (tenant, destination) exceeds configurable max_lag_seconds for a sustained window, we stop pulling new batches for that destination and mark it PAUSED (reason=MAX_LAG) while continuing to process other destinations/tenants. (2) Backpressure based on in-flight bytes and retry backlog: introduce per-destination in_flight_bytes limits...

title: Bootstrap audit exporter service skeleton + CI + local dev harness
preview:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; ...
text:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; those will follow in subsequent PRs.

## Motivation / Context
We need a dedicated service to read audit events (initially via stubbed source in local dev), apply tenant scoping, and deliver to downstream destinations with retries/checkpointing. Before we can iterate on architecture and adapters, we need:
- Standard project layout + build tooling
- Baseline CI (lint/test/build, container build)
- A config model (typed) that can evolve with control-plane config sync
- A local harness so devs can run the service end-to-end without needing the full control plane

## What’s incl...

title: Add dashboards + alerts for data residency enforcement (blocked requests, policy eval latency)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a first-pass set of dashboards and alerting rules for Hosted data residency enforcement. The focus is on (1) visibility into enforcement outcomes (allowed vs blocked), (2) early detection of regressions in policy evaluation latency, and (3) detecting cross-region routing attempts that should be...
text: Summary: This PR adds a first-pass set of dashboards and alerting rules for Hosted data residency enforcement. The focus is on (1) visibility into enforcement outcomes (allowed vs blocked), (2) early detection of regressions in policy evaluation latency, and (3) detecting cross-region routing attempts that should be prevented once enforcement is enabled. Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement rollout (region pinning + deterministic request-time blocking), SRE and Security asked for a consistent set of metrics visualizations and actionable alerts. This pack is intended to be deployed alongside the routing/gateway changes and used during dogfood and progressive rollout. What’s included: Grafana dashboard: “Data Residency Enforcement” with panels: Overview: total requests, allowed/blocked, block-rate (%), and top block reasons (5m/1h). Blocked requests by region + endpoint...

title: Add Datadog Logs destination adapter + audit tag/facet mapping
preview: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preservi...
text: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preserving at-least-once semantics. Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we support Splunk HEC, S3, and Datadog Logs. Datadog differs from Splunk in that (a) intake is strongly rate-limited, and (b) the best user experience is via consistent tagging/faceting for filtering/dashboards in Datadog. This PR aligns with ENG-5156 and the delivery semantics ADR (at-least-once, downstream dedup via event_id). Design notes (high-level): Adapter: Adds `datadog_logs` destination type with `site`, `intake_path`, and `api_key_secret_ref`. We send batches to `https://http-intake....

title: Routing: enforce residency region pinning + disable cross-region fallback by default
preview: Summary: Integrates Hosted Data Residency “region pinning” policy evaluation into Smart Routing decisions. Requests are constrained to the policy’s allowed_regions and will no longer fail over cross-region by default. Adds structured decision context to routing logs/spans for traceability and downstream error shapin...
text:
Summary: Integrates Hosted Data Residency “region pinning” policy evaluation into Smart Routing decisions. Requests are constrained to the policy’s allowed_regions and will no longer fail over cross-region by default. Adds structured decision context to routing logs/spans for traceability and downstream error shaping.

Why: We’ve had customer incidents where routing attempted cross-region fallback when a customer expected EU-only processing (see SUP-28441). This PR ensures routing respects residency constraints deterministically, and makes the decision observable (selected region, policy source, and block reason).

Behavior changes: 1) Policy evaluation: Router calls ResidencyPolicyService (cached) at request admission to resolve the effective residency policy (API key > project > org precedence). 2) Region selection: Candidate regions list is intersected with policy.allowed_regions. ...

title: Add Splunk HEC destination adapter (gzip compression, 429 backoff, per-token circuit breaker)
preview: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persiste...
text: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persistent throttling. Motivation: Beta customers are primarily Splunk-first and we observed exporter lag spikes when HEC returns sustained 429s; this change improves delivery stability and reduces noisy retry behavior. Scope: Adapter implementation + config schema + basic unit tests + metrics hooks. Not included: Splunk indexer acknowledgment (HEC ACK) support (will track separately), advanced event field remapping beyond the audit schema’s SIEM mapping guidance. Delivery semantics: remains at-least-once; downstream Splunk consumers should dedupe on event_id if needed. Config: add...

title: Add dead-letter queue + guarded replay CLI for failed export batches
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently d...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently drop events while still protecting the platform and customers from accidental replay storms. Context / Motivation: During downstream outages (e.g., sustained 429s from Splunk HEC or 403s from mis-scoped Datadog keys), the exporter currently retries with backoff and then surfaces a per-destination error. Without DLQ persistence we risk either (a) losing batches on process/queue churn or (b) having no safe operational path to replay specific failures once a customer fixes credentials/quotas. ENG-5164 calls for a DLQ + replay tooling with explicit operator controls. Design high...

title: Support backup-oriented batch export mode (pagination + retention guardrails)
preview: Context: Private DR / compliance asks require a repeatable, non-interactive way to export audit logs for backup/retention evidence. The existing exporter is optimized for ad-hoc, interactive exports and can be slow/fragile when used in scheduled jobs (offset-based pagination, no checkpointing, limited safeguards aro...
text:
Context: Private DR / compliance asks require a repeatable, non-interactive way to export audit logs for backup/retention evidence. The existing exporter is optimized for ad-hoc, interactive exports and can be slow/fragile when used in scheduled jobs (offset-based pagination, no checkpointing, limited safeguards around retention boundaries).

This PR adds a backup-oriented batch export mode designed for scheduled backups:
- New CLI subcommand: `audit-log-exporter export batch`
- Cursor-based pagination using the API’s stable `after_id`/`next_cursor` semantics (no offset)
- Checkpointing/resume: writes a checkpoint token to disk periodically; supports `--resume` to continue without duplicating output
- Retention guardrails: validates requested time window against server-reported retention (if available) and fails fast with actionable messaging; explicit override flag required to procee...

title: Emit metrics + tracing for Private backup/restore operations (with error categorization)
preview: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds...
text: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds: (1) structured Prometheus metrics for backup + restore operations (success/failure, duration, bytes, last success timestamps), (2) OpenTelemetry tracing spans with consistent attributes for per-step visibility, and (3) explicit error categorization to drive dashboards/alerts and reduce pager noise. Scope: This instruments the control-plane backup/restore path (installer-driven + control-plane endpoints) and adds common helpers used by both “backup create” and “restore apply”. It does not change backup artifact format or encryption behavior. Metrics (Prometheus): - redwood...

title: Private: expose backup status endpoints from control plane
preview: Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with ...
text:
Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with strict authz and safe redaction (no bucket names, object keys, or secret material). This unblocks Console UI + automated restore drills to gate on “recent successful backup” and to page on backup job regressions.

What’s included: Adds a BackupStatus read model persisted by the backup orchestrator after each run (success/failure), keyed by tenant_id and environment_id; Read-only endpoints under the Private API surface; Includes timestamps (last_success_at, last_attempt_at), status enum, last_error_category + last_error_message (truncated), artifact metadata (format_version,...

title: Implement S3 destination adapter (batching, partitioning, checkpoints, retries)
preview:
Context: This PR adds the first destination adapter for the SIEM-ready audit export beta: S3 delivery. The adapter is built around (a) deterministic partitioning for cost/operability, (b) bounded batching for throughput, and (c) at-least-once delivery via checkpoint persistence.

Linear: ENG-5144

What’s included:
-...
text:
Context: This PR adds the first destination adapter for the SIEM-ready audit export beta: S3 delivery. The adapter is built around (a) deterministic partitioning for cost/operability, (b) bounded batching for throughput, and (c) at-least-once delivery via checkpoint persistence.

Linear: ENG-5144

What’s included:
- New S3 destination adapter implementing the exporter Destination interface
- Batching policy: flush on max_events, max_bytes, or flush_interval (defaults tuned for beta)
- Partitioning scheme (configurable prefix + time partitioning + tenant scoping) to avoid hot prefixes and keep search-friendly layouts
- Checkpoint persistence for at-least-once semantics (write object -> persist checkpoint). Cursor is (emitted_at, event_id) and is treated as an opaque ordering token by downstream
- Retry/backoff for S3 throttling/transient errors (SlowDown/503/timeouts) with jitter and a...

title: Normalize region identifiers in metrics pipeline (canonical mapping + normalized_region tag)
preview: Context: The Unit Economics dashboard (ENG-8426) requires stable grouping by region, but today we emit a mix of cloud region strings (e.g., "us-east-1"), internal colo labels (e.g., "iad-1"), and provider-specific variants (e.g., "eu-west" vs "eu-west-1"). This causes dropped rows in aggregation (joins fail), duplic...
text:
Context: The Unit Economics dashboard (ENG-8426) requires stable grouping by region, but today we emit a mix of cloud region strings (e.g., "us-east-1"), internal colo labels (e.g., "iad-1"), and provider-specific variants (e.g., "eu-west" vs "eu-west-1"). This causes dropped rows in aggregation (joins fail), duplicate region groups in dashboards, and inconsistent reconciliation vs Finance rate cards.

This PR introduces a canonical region mapping and updates ingestion so analytics receives a single, normalized region dimension.

What’s included: (1) Canonical region mapping table + versioned source-of-truth (2) Ingestion changes to emit `normalized_region` (and preserve raw `region` for debugging) (3) Guardrails for unknown/unmapped regions (4) Backfill-friendly behavior and unit tests.

Design notes: - The mapping is intentionally small and stable; we do NOT map down to per-AZ. - We...
